title: Github pages
content:
# Deploy to GitHub Pages

This document will guide you step-by-step on how to deploy your Kecare blog to GitHub Pages, taking your site from local to online.

---

## Prerequisites

Before starting the deployment, please ensure you have the following ready:

| Preparation Item | Description |
|--------|------|
| GitHub Account | If you don't have one, please [Sign up for GitHub](https://github.com/signup) first |
| Git | Git is installed locally, can be verified via `git --version` |
| Node.js | Node.js is installed locally, used for installing dependencies and building the project |

---

## Create a GitHub Repository

### Step 1: Log in to GitHub

Visit [GitHub](https://github.com) and log in to your account.

### Step 2: Create a New Repository

1. Click the **+** button in the upper right corner and select **New repository**
2. Fill in the repository information:
   - **Repository name**: Repository name, customizable (e.g., `my-blog`)
   - **Visibility**: Select **Public** (public), GitHub Pages free tier only supports public repositories
3. Click **Create repository** to complete the creation

### Step 3: Record the Repository Address

After creation, you will see the repository address in the following format:

```txt
https://github.com/<username>/<repo>.git
```

Or SSH format:

```txt
git@github.com:<username>/<repo>.git
```

---

## Configuring SSH Connection

> 💡 **Tip**: If you have already configured SSH, you can skip this step.

### Generate SSH Key

Open the terminal and execute the following commands:

```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"
```

Press Enter three times to use the default settings.

### Add Public Key to GitHub

1. View the public key content:
   ```bash
   cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
   ```

2. Copy the output content (starting with `ssh-ed25519`)

3. Add on GitHub:
   - Go to **Settings** → **SSH and GPG keys**
   - Click **New SSH key**
   - Paste the public key content and save

> 📖 **Detailed Tutorial**: [GitHub Official Documentation - Adding an SSH Key](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/connecting-to-github-with-ssh/adding-a-new-ssh-key-to-your-github-account)

### Verify SSH Connection

```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
```

The first connection will prompt you to confirm the fingerprint. Type `yes`. Seeing the welcome message indicates success.

---

## Push Code to GitHub

### Initialize Git Repository (if not already initialized)

```bash
cd <你的主题目录>
git init
git add -A
git commit -m "init: 初始化项目"
```

### Add Remote Repository and Push

Replace `<username>` and `<repo>` with your GitHub username and repository name:

```bash
git branch -M main
git remote add origin git@github.com:<username>/<repo>.git
git push -u origin main
```

After a successful push, your code will appear in the `main` branch of the GitHub repository.

---

## Configuring Nuxt Build

> ⚠️ **Note**: This section is for the example theme using Nuxt. If you are using other frameworks, please refer to the deployment documentation for the corresponding framework.

GitHub Pages only supports static sites. Nuxt requires pre-rendering the application into static HTML files.

### Configuring Base URL

If you are not using a custom domain, you need to set the correct Base URL.

**Method 1: Configure in nuxt.config.ts**

```ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  compatibilityDate: '2025-07-15',
  devtools: { enabled: true },
  app: {
    baseURL: '/<repository>/',  // 替换为你的仓库名
    head: {
      // ...
    },
  },
});
```

**Method 2: Configure via Environment Variables**

```bash
NUXT_APP_BASE_URL=/<repository>/
```

> 📖 **Reference**: [Nuxt Official Documentation - GitHub Pages Deployment](https://nuxt.com/deploy/github-pages)

---

## Build and Publish

### Install Dependencies

Make sure to execute in the **theme directory**:

```bash
npm install
```

### Building Static Files

```bash
npx nuxt build --preset github_pages
```

After the build is complete, static files will be output to:

```txt
.output/public/
```

### Publish to the gh-pages branch

Using the `gh-pages` tool to push the build artifacts to the `gh-pages` branch:

```bash
npx gh-pages -d .output/public
```

This command will automatically create the `gh-pages` branch and push the static files.

---

## Enable GitHub Pages

### Configuring Pages Settings

1. Go to your GitHub repository
2. Click on **Settings**
3. Find **Pages** in the left-hand menu
4. Configure the publishing source:
   - **Source**: Select **Deploy from a branch**
   - **Branch**: Select **gh-pages**
   - **Folder**: Select **/ (root)**
5. Click **Save**

### Access Your Site

After a brief moment, GitHub will provide your site address:

```txt
https://<username>.github.io/<repo>/
```

Click the link to visit your blog!

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Q: How to achieve automatic deployment?

A: You can use GitHub Actions to achieve automatic build and deployment. Nuxt officially provides example workflows. For details, refer to the [Nuxt GitHub Pages Deployment Documentation](https://nuxt.com/deploy/github-pages).

### Q: How to use a custom domain?

A: Add a custom domain in GitHub Pages settings and configure DNS resolution at your domain service provider to point to GitHub Pages.

### Q: What to do if the build fails?

A: I don't know

Your blog is now live and ready to be shared! 🎉
